contact.heading: We're here to help
contact.description: Have questions about diabetes prevention, obesity management, or treatments in Pakistan? Please reach out. Your journey to better health starts with a conversation.
contact.subject: Subject
contact.success: Thank you! Your message has been sent successfully.
contact.error: Sorry, there was an error sending your message. Please try again.

hba1c.heroDescription: Convert between HbA1c (%) and estimated average glucose (eAG). HbA1c reflects your average blood sugar over the past 2-3 months.
hba1c.note: Note: These values are estimates. Actual blood glucose can vary. Always consult your healthcare provider for interpretation.

errors.invalidHba1c: Please enter a valid HbA1c value.
errors.hba1cRange: HbA1c value should be between 3% and 20%.
errors.invalidGlucose: Please enter a valid glucose value.

descriptions.normal: Your blood sugar levels are within the normal range. Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle.
descriptions.prediabetes: Your blood sugar levels indicate prediabetes. Lifestyle changes can help prevent progression to diabetes.
descriptions.diabetes: Your blood sugar levels are in the diabetes range. Please consult with your healthcare provider for proper management.

calorie.heroDescription: Search any food item to see its calorie content and nutritional breakdown. Adjust the quantity to match your serving.

bmi.heroDescription: Body Mass Index (BMI) is a simple screening tool that uses your height and weight to estimate body fat. Enter your details below to calculate your BMI.

errors.ageTooYoung: BMI calculation is not reliable for children under 2 years old.

result.prompt: Enter your details and click "Calculate BMI" to see your result.

descriptions.underweight: A BMI below 18.5 may indicate insufficient body weight. Consider consulting a healthcare provider about nutrition.
descriptions.normal: Your BMI is within the healthy range. Maintain your current lifestyle with balanced nutrition and regular activity.
descriptions.overweight: A BMI of 23.5–24.9 is considered overweight. Small lifestyle changes in diet and activity can help reduce future health risks.
descriptions.obese1: A BMI of 25–29.9 is classified as Class I obesity. Lifestyle changes and medical guidance can support better health.
descriptions.obese2: A BMI of 30–34.9 is classified as Class II obesity. Please consult a healthcare provider for personalised advice.
descriptions.obese3: A BMI of 35 or above is classified as Class III obesity. Medical support is recommended for safe, effective management.
descriptions.child: For children and teens (ages 2–17), BMI is interpreted using age and gender-specific percentiles. Please consult a paediatrician for a full assessment.

info.heading: Understanding Your BMI
info.note: Note: BMI is a screening tool, not a diagnostic measure, and does not account for muscle mass or bone density. Always consult a healthcare professional for a comprehensive assessment.

s1.heading: A new chapter in diabesity care
s1.body: Explore how modern medical science — including GLP-1–based therapies — is reshaping the understanding of obesity and type 2 diabetes.

s2.heading: Take control of your health today
s2.body: Discover how modern treatments like GLP-1s are helping Pakistanis manage weight and blood sugar like never before.

s1.title: Understand diabesity
s1.body: Gain a clear understanding of the link between type 2 diabetes and obesity, including diabesity, GLP-1 biology, and modern treatment pathways.

s2.title: Eat smart and healthy
s2.body: You don't have to sacrifice flavour for health. Explore delicious recipes and meal-planning tips that make healthy eating simple and enjoyable.

s3.title: Daily care & monitoring
s3.body: Consistent self-care is key to managing your condition. Find practical guides to track blood sugar, recognise symptoms, and support day-to-day diabetes care.

stat1.label: adults with undiagnosed diabetes
stat1.description: In Pakistan, a quarter of adults with diabetes don't know they have it, allowing silent progression to severe complications before the first symptom is even noticed.

stat2.label: heart attack patients have diabetes
stat2.description: A staggering 6 out of 10 heart attack patients in Pakistan also have diabetes, highlighting the critical link between unmanaged blood sugar and cardiovascular risk.

stat3.label: million (USD)
stat3.description: The annual cost of diabetes care in Pakistan exceeded $2.6 billion in 2021, placing an immense financial burden on families and the national healthcare system.

support.heading: Diabesity support in your area
support.description: Find regional contact information, your local support groups and activities near you.

stories.heading: Stories: This is Diabesity
stories.description: These are the unfiltered voices of people navigating life with diabesity. Read their powerful, personal journeys of challenge, discovery, and hope to find inspiration and a shared understanding.
stories.author: ~ Zainab Jan

video.description1: What is diabetes? Find out in our two-minute video spoken in English.
video.description2Prefix: Or choose a version in Urdu, Gujarati, Punjabi or Sylheti from our

glp.description: Learn how appetite regulation, insulin signalling, and metabolic health are connected.

community.description: Read about the community support we offer for people living with or affected by diabesity

result.lowRiskDesc2: If you believe you may be at risk or have been diagnosed with diabetes, please consult with your healthcare provider.

result.disclaimer: This is an estimate based on common patterns. Only a medical professional can provide an accurate diagnosis. You can adjust the diabetes type in the next step.

hero.heading: The GLP-1 Hub
hero.description: Understanding how GLP-1 connects appetite, blood sugar, and weight management. Discover the science behind this powerful hormone and how it can help address diabesity.

diet.title: Supporting GLP-1 naturally
diet.description: Learn how diet and lifestyle choices can support your body's natural GLP-1 production.

medication.title: Restoring GLP-1 with treatment
medication.description: Understand the medical therapies available to restore and enhance GLP-1 signaling.

tools.title: Measuring GLP-1 outcomes
tools.description: Track your progress with the right tools and understand what the numbers mean.

hero.heading: How To
hero.description: Step-by-step guides for daily living with diabesity. From starting your journey to handling social events, reading food labels, and managing medications—practical advice you can use today.

exercise.title: How to Exercise at Home — Move more, safely

readLabels.title: How to Read Food Labels — Pakistani brands made simple

socialEvents.title: How to Handle Social Events & Cravings — Eid, weddings, office treats

prescriptionTreatments.title: How to Use Prescription Treatments — Pens, pills, and practical tips

bloodSugar.title: How to Handle High or Low Blood Sugar — Calm, clear actions

termsOfUse.s1Body: Welcome to Diabesity. By accessing and using this website, you accept and agree to be bound by the terms and provisions of this agreement. If you do not agree to abide by these terms, please do not use this website. These terms apply to all visitors, users, and others who access or use the website.
termsOfUse.s2Body1: The information provided on this website is for general informational and educational purposes only and is not intended as a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always seek the advice of your physician or other qualified healthcare provider with any questions you may have regarding a medical condition.
termsOfUse.s2Body2: Never disregard professional medical advice or delay in seeking it because of something you have read on this website. If you think you may have a medical emergency, call your doctor or emergency services immediately. Diabesity does not recommend or endorse any specific tests, physicians, products, procedures, opinions, or other information that may be mentioned on this website.
termsOfUse.s3Body1: All content on this website, including text, graphics, logos, images, and software, is the property of Diabesity or its content suppliers and is protected by Pakistani and international copyright laws. You may not reproduce, distribute, modify, create derivative works of, publicly display, or otherwise use any content without prior written permission.
termsOfUse.s3Body2: You are permitted to view, download, and print pages from this website for your own personal, non-commercial use, provided you do not modify the content and retain all copyright and proprietary notices.
termsOfUse.s4Intro: When using this website, you agree not to:
termsOfUse.s4li0: Use the website for any unlawful purpose or in violation of any applicable laws of Pakistan
termsOfUse.s4li1: Attempt to gain unauthorized access to any portion of the website or any systems or networks connected to the website
termsOfUse.s4li2: Interfere with or disrupt the website or servers or networks connected to the website
termsOfUse.s4li3: Upload or transmit any viruses, malware, or other harmful code
termsOfUse.s4li4: Collect or store personal data about other users without their consent
termsOfUse.s4li5: Use the website to send unsolicited communications or spam
termsOfUse.s5Body: This website may contain links to third-party websites or services that are not owned or controlled by Diabesity. We have no control over, and assume no responsibility for, the content, privacy policies, or practices of any third-party websites or services. You acknowledge and agree that Diabesity shall not be responsible or liable for any damage or loss caused by or in connection with the use of any such content, goods, or services available on or through any such websites or services.
termsOfUse.s6Body: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, Diabesity shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of profits, data, or other intangible losses, resulting from your access to or use of (or inability to access or use) the website, any conduct or content of any third party on the website, or any content obtained from the website.
termsOfUse.s7Body: You agree to defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Diabesity and its officers, directors, employees, and agents from and against any claims, liabilities, damages, losses, and expenses, including reasonable legal fees, arising out of or in any way connected with your access to or use of the website or your violation of these Terms of Use.
termsOfUse.s8Body: We reserve the right, at our sole discretion, to modify or replace these Terms of Use at any time. If a revision is material, we will try to provide at least 30 days' notice prior to any new terms taking effect. What constitutes a material change will be determined at our sole discretion. By continuing to access or use our website after those revisions become effective, you agree to be bound by the revised terms.
termsOfUse.s9Body: These Terms of Use shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Pakistan, without regard to its conflict of law provisions. Any disputes arising under or in connection with these terms shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Pakistan.
termsOfUse.s10Body: If you have any questions about these Terms of Use, please contact us through our website's contact page or email us at the address provided in the footer of our website.

privacyPolicy.s1Heading: Information that is gathered from visitors
privacyPolicy.s1Body: In common with other websites, log files are stored on the web server saving details such as the visitor's IP address, browser type, referring page and time of visit. Cookies may be used to remember visitor preferences when interacting with the website. Where registration is required, the visitor's email, and a username will be stored on the server.
privacyPolicy.s2Body: The information is used to enhance the visitor's experience when using the website to display personalized content and possibly advertising. E-mail addresses will not be sold, rented or leased to 3rd parties. E-mail may be sent to inform you of news of our services or offers by us or our affiliates.
privacyPolicy.s3Body: If you have subscribed to one of our services, you may unsubscribe by following the instructions which are included in the email that you receive. You may be able to block cookies via your browser settings but this may prevent you from access to certain features of the website.
privacyPolicy.s4Body: Cookies are small digital signature files that are stored by your web browser that allow your preferences to be recorded when visiting the website. Also, they may be used to track your return visits to the website. 3rd party advertising companies may also use cookies for tracking purposes.

hero.heading: Health is your fundamental right
hero.body: We are a coalition of doctors, nutritionists, patients, and families who believe every Pakistani deserves the knowledge and tools to live a life free from the grip of diabesity. This is our promise to you and to Pakistan.

story.heading: Our Story
story.p1: This devastating link between diabetes and obesity, is not a statistic. It's our parents, our siblings, our friends.
story.p2: We saw a nation grappling with the world's highest rate of diabetes, yet facing a wall of misinformation, stigma, and resources that didn't fit our culture or our lives. The advice was generic, the meal plans were foreign, and the conversation was happening in whispers, if at all.
story.p3: We knew we couldn't stand by while this silent epidemic stole the health and futures of millions.
story.p4Prefix: So, we built the solution we needed.
story.p4Bold: Diabesity Pakistan is your clear path forward.
story.p4Suffix: It was founded to bridge the gaps, to replace confusion with clarity, and to empower you with guidance tailored for the realities of life in Pakistan.

aboutUs.heading: About us and our battle against 'Diabesity'
aboutUs.body: We are a motivated group of healthcare professionals, civil society activists, and concerned citizens who share a common purpose of helping Pakistan fight the growing diabetes and obesity pandemic, also now being increasingly known as 'Diabesity'.
aboutUs.list0: Connect individuals to resources, specialists, and support systems.
aboutUs.list1: Promote healthy living grounded in our culture and day-to-day realities.
aboutUs.list2: Strive to eliminate stigma associated with diabesity and provide everyone with the resources to lead healthy lives.
aboutUs.list3: Deliver reliable information in English and Urdu to raise awareness on diabesity.
aboutUs.closingPrefix: For the benefit of current and future generations, we invite you to
aboutUs.closingSuffix: yourself and your loved ones, and participate in a

mission.label: Key Objectives
mission.heading: Our Mission in Action
mission.card0Desc: We break down complex topics like managing blood sugar to understanding medications into simple, actionable guides in both Urdu and English. We believe everyone has the right to understand their own health.
mission.card1Desc: We create diet plans that celebrate Pakistani cuisine by providing expert advice that understands your reality, from Lahore to the most remote village. These are resources designed to work with you.
mission.card2Desc: You are not alone on this journey. We share real stories from Pakistanis just like you to fight stigma, build a national network of support, and prove that change is possible together.
mission.card3Desc: Our ultimate goal is to turn the tide on this epidemic. By empowering individuals and families today, we are working to ensure that future generations of Pakistanis can live longer, stronger, and healthier lives.
